title: Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Karnataka India, which was founded to protect chinkaras (Indian Gazelle).
overview.description: Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Karnataka India, which was founded to protect chinkaras (Indian Gazelle). Established in 2019, it is the second chinkara sanctuary in Karnataka after Yadahalli Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.description: Bukkapatna is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sira taluk of Tumkur district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (115 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (116 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bukkapatna Chinkara Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
